(Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きぬまきじんじゃ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名きぬまきじんじゃ]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

(Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名きぬまきじんじゃ]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * きぬまきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6140005012137 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Amano Shrine|lt=Amano Shrine|WD=Q135040693|1=Amano Shrine (Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * きぬまきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6140005012137 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Amano Shrine|lt=Amano Shrine|WD=Q135040693|1=Amano Shrine (Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * きぬまきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6140005012137 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Amano Shrine|lt=Amano Shrine|WD=Q135040693|1=Amano Shrine (Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

(Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * きぬまきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6140005012137 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Amano Shrine|lt=Amano Shrine|WD=Q135040693|1=Amano Shrine (Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

(Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * きぬまきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6140005012137 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Amano Shrine|lt=Amano Shrine|WD=Q135040693|1=Amano Shrine (Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

(Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|WD=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|WD=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|WD=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|WD=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|WD=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|WD=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|WD=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|WD=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|WD=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|WD=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

(Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|qid=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|qid=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|qid=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|qid=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|qid=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|qid=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|qid=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|qid=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|qid=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|qid=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|qid=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|qid=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]]

(Q135040693)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県豊岡市気比字絹巻2585番地1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:絹巻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21655263|ja|絹巻神社}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kinumaki Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Kinumaki 20090621155520.jpg|270px|Kinumaki Shrine grove]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|qid=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}Kebi, Aza Kinumaki 2585-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} 10 (if following Kojiki sexagenary correction, {{ill|lt=322|Q34068|qid=Q34068|1=322}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 28|Q2534|qid=Q2534|1=April 28}} |札所等 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kinumaki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kinumakijinja1.jpg|thumb|Kinumaki Shrine haiden and honden]] '''Kinumaki Shrine''' (きぬまきじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Toyooka|Q696388|qid=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is identified with Watatsumi Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). It is regarded as one of the five major shrines of Tajima. === Overview === An old shrine counted among the ten myojin taisha of Tajima Province. Its principal deity, Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto, is a deity of sunlight and heat. In ''Kojikiden'', "hoakari" is interpreted as "ripe ears turning red," and as with other deities linked to imperial lineage, is connected with rice, so the deity is worshipped as both sun and agricultural god. It is the same deity as enshrined at Moto-Ise Kono Shrine in Miyazu, ichinomiya of Tango Province, where the principal deity is named Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko-Hoakari-no-Mikoto. === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deity :* {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html |title=Kinumaki Shrine |publisher=Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2016-06-18}} ; Co-enshrined deities :* Ama-no-Atae-no-Mikoto :* Ama-no-Orihime-no-Mikoto === History === "Kinumaki Shrine Origin" states: Enshrined deities: Upper seat: Taketase-no-Mikoto Middle seat: Amateru-kuni-teru-hiko Ame-no-Hoakari Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto Lower seat: Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto In ''Kokushi Monjo, Butsuma Shrine Genealogical Tradition'': Tayui-gō, Watatsumi Shrine, enshrined on Mt. Kinumaki in Kinosaki District Deities: Upper seat: Wadatsumi-no-Kami (moved from Watatsumi shrine at Oda'i county) Middle seat: Sumiyoshi deity (moved from Sumiyoshi deity at Kamikura-yama) Lower seat: Ama-no-Atae, also called Ōama no Hiko-no-Mikoto Ama-no-Atae-hime-no-Mikoto, also called Ōama no Hime-no-Mikoto Another tradition says: Kushihikata-no-Mikoto / Tamakushi-hime-no-Mikoto Kebi district chief Seto no Sukune invited Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Watatsumi-no-Kami, and Funadama-no-Kami, enshrined them, and appointed shrine attendants, with Kebi Yoshimaro serving rites. ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (Jōwa 9, 842): Five shrines in Tajima Province (Keta District Yama, Ikazuchi, To, Hajikami and Kinosaki District Watatsumi) were entered into official shrine status. ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (Jōgan 10, 868): Promotion of deities including Tajima's Watatsumi to Junior Fifth Rank Upper. ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima's myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. === About Tajima Province government-offering shrines === ''Engishiki'' temporary rites, myojin festival section: Lists Tajima myojin shrines including Watatsumi Shrine. Waka by Emperor Go-Daigo: "Look up, in the Oda'i forest traces remain, deep too the blessings of Watatsumi deity." Waka of Kinumaki Daimyōjin, foremost of the five shrines: "Cutting through the strait, the sword stayed in the chest, now made divine in Kebi shrine-land." === Subsidiary shrine === Inari Shrine === Rituals === * Annual festival - April 28 === Location information === ; Location :* Kebi Aza Kinumaki 2585-1, Toyooka, Hyogo Prefecture ; Access :* Bus :** From {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|Q1055689|qid=Q1055689|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} ({{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=San'in Main Line|Q1135170|qid=Q1135170|1=San'in Main Line}}), get off at Kojima ({{ill|lt=Inakā|Q11286876|qid=Q11286876|1=Inakā}} Kebi-Mihara line / {{ill|lt=Zentan Bus|Q11389110|qid=Q11389110|1=Zentan Bus}} Hiyoriyama line), walk across Seto bridge 450 m (about 5 min). :* Road :** About 2.7 km (about 5 min) from Kinosaki Onsen via {{ill|lt=Hyogo Route 3 Toyooka-Seto Line|Q11393412|qid=Q11393412|1=Hyogo Prefectural Road Route 3}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Marine World|Q11426136|qid=Q11426136|1=Kinosaki Marine World}} * {{ill|lt=Kinosaki Onsen|Q11426141|qid=Q11426141|1=Kinosaki Onsen}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|Q11381626|qid=Q11381626|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322080.html Kinumaki Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinumaki Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]]
